description_long: This object is a ceiling-mounted electric fan with three elongated, flat blades radiating from a central hub. The blades are evenly spaced and have a slightly tapered design, giving the fan a modern and utilitarian appearance. The central hub is cylindrical and connects to a short downrod, which would attach to a ceiling. The entire fan is finished in a uniform dark gray color, suggesting a metallic or coated metal construction. There are no visible blade guards, control panels, or decorative elements, emphasizing a minimalist and functional design. The fan is designed to circulate air efficiently in indoor or semi-outdoor urban environments such as garages, workshops, or public spaces.
description: A minimalist, dark gray ceiling-mounted electric fan with three flat, evenly spaced blades and a cylindrical central hub. The design is functional and modern, with no visible blade guard or control panel.
description_view_0: The fan is seen from an angled top-down perspective, clearly showing all three blades radiating from the central hub.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the thickness and flatness of the blades, as well as the short downrod connecting the fan to the ceiling.
description_view_2: Another angled view, similar to view 0 but rotated, again displaying the three blades and the central hub.
description_view_3: A direct side view, emphasizing the length of one blade and the cylindrical shape of the central hub and downrod.
attribute_blade_guard_design: No blade guard is present; the blades are fully exposed with no mesh, grille, or protective structure.
